We ourselves are not our own; our bodies, our souls, are not. Even those of other people are God's, who do not know him, nor acknowledge their relationship to him. A soul that knows and considers its own nature, and that it must live forever, when it has seen the earth and all that is in it, will remain unsatisfied. It will think of ascending toward God, and will ask, What shall I do, that I may dwell in that happy, holy place, where he makes his people holy and happy? We accomplish nothing with religion if we do not make it a matter of our hearts. We can only be cleansed from our sins, and renewed to holiness, by the blood of Christ and the washing of the Holy Spirit. Thus we become his people; thus we receive blessing from the Lord, and righteousness from the God of our salvation. God's special people will be made truly and forever happy. Where God gives righteousness, he intends salvation. Those who are prepared for heaven will be safely brought to heaven, and will find what they have been seeking.

The magnificent entrance described here refers to the solemn bringing in of the ark into the tent David pitched for it, or the temple Solomon built for it. We can also apply it to Christ’s ascension into heaven, and the welcome given to him there. Our Redeemer found the gates of heaven shut, but having by his blood made atonement for sin, as one having authority, he demanded entrance. The angels were to worship him; they ask with wonder, Who is he? It is answered that he is strong and mighty; mighty in battle to save his people, and to subdue his and their enemies. We can apply it to Christ’s entrance into the souls of men by his word and Spirit, that they may be his temples. Behold, he stands at the door, and knocks. The gates and doors of the heart are to be opened to him, as possession is delivered to the rightful owner. We can apply it to his second coming with glorious power. Lord, open the everlasting door of our souls by your grace, that we may now receive you, and be wholly yours; and that, eventually, we may be numbered with your saints in glory.
